title: Fix admin menu item visibility in UserDropdown

requirements.summary: Fix the Admin panel link in UserDropdown so it reliably appears for authenticated admin users by auditing and correcting the full chain: backend admin check, useIsCallerAdmin hook with authenticated identity, React Query cache behavior, and conditional rendering logic
acceptanceCriteria: After logging in with an admin Internet Identity, the 'Admin' menu item is visible in the UserDropdown without requiring a page refresh.
Non-admin authenticated users do not see the Admin menu item.
Unauthenticated users do not see the Admin menu item.
Navigating to /admin via the dropdown link works correctly for admin users.
The useIsCallerAdmin() hook uses the authenticated actor (not the anonymous actor) when querying the backend.

file_operations.description: Fix the useIsCallerAdmin hook to ensure it uses the authenticated actor and properly depends on actor availability. Add actorFetching to the enabled condition so the query only runs when the actor is fully ready. Update the query key to include a dependency on authentication state to ensure cache invalidation after login. Return a custom isLoading state that accounts for actor loading: isLoading should be true when actorFetching is true OR when the query itself is loading.

Update the conditional rendering logic for the Admin menu item to account for the loading state of the useIsCallerAdmin hook. Show the Admin link only when isCallerAdmin is true AND the hook is not loading. Ensure the component waits for both authentication and the admin check to complete before deciding whether to show the Admin link. This prevents premature hiding of the link due to stale cache or incomplete queries.
